reviews.review_text:
You shoot da gun, swing da sword, pew pew da spells, summon friends, and then there is yo-yos.
Yo-yos are dope = 10/10 game.

The building is incredibly fun and complex if you want it to be. Bosses are peak. They never stopped updating even despite saying they were going to years ago. Modding community is amazing if you want more (which IS NOT necessary, the base game has soooo much.)

Find myself coming back to Terraria every year with how amazing it is.

terraria, oh where do i even start...

At first glance, Terraria looks like “2D Minecraft,” but that comparison honestly doesn’t do it justice. The game evolves from chopping trees and building tiny wooden houses into fighting screen-filling gods, exploring corrupted biomes, crafting hundreds of weapons, and slowly becoming an unstoppable force of destruction. The progression system is insanely satisfying because every boss unlocks new materials, stronger gear, harder enemies, and completely changes how the game feels.

The exploration is phenomenal too. Every world feels alive with hidden caves, floating islands, underground jungles, deserts, oceans, temples, corruption/crimson biomes, and secrets buried literally everywhere. The “just one more cave” effect is dangerously real. I’ve lost hours mining because I kept finding something new.

The boss design is another massive highlight. Early bosses feel manageable, but later fights become absolute chaos in the best way possible. Dodging lasers, explosions, flying swords, and giant eldritch horrors while your screen turns into a fireworks show never stops being exciting.The soundtrack deserves praise too. Some tracks are weirdly relaxing while others perfectly capture the tension of boss fights and dangerous biomes. The music becomes surprisingly nostalgic after enough hours.

Multiplayer makes everything even better. Building giant bases with friends, preparing arenas for bosses, and accidentally summoning something horrific before everyone is ready creates some of the funniest gaming moments imaginable.

At this point, Terraria feels less like a game and more like a massive adventure sandbox that somehow keeps rewarding curiosity no matter how long you play.

I used to talk so much ♥♥♥♥ about this game. "It’s just 2D Minecraft," I’d say, scoffing from my 3D high horse.

Well, my opinions have changed... slightly. It is still just 2D Minecraft, but it turns out 2D Minecraft goes incredibly hard. The lore is wild, the bosses are genuinely insane, and the building experience is way more addictive than it has any right to be.

Back in the day, the boys and I loaded this thing up with so many mods it practically turned into a different game, and we had an absolute blast. I came for the memes, but I stayed for the Cthulhu eyes and endless grinding.

10/10. Will continue to call it 2D Minecraft while putting another few hours of my life into it.

I've been gaming for 40 years now and Terraria is a top tier game for sure.  I think everyone should try it and give it a good playing before putting it away.  It's a game I think people should always have installed.

Kudos to devs, still update after all these years. Paid once, lifetime support. (and cheap too) Thank you for being so generous, modern day games mostly infested with micro transactions, plenty of paid DLC. I love co-op but most of them comes with greedy DLC, and that’s a major turnoff.

My first experience of Terraria was a cracked alpha file I found on some shady website in late 2011. At the time, the Wall of Flesh had just been added, and it was more than enough for my young mind to content with. I remember staying up all night til sunrise playing that janky version of the game, and adoring it.

Over the years I played the 360 when it released, and the xbox one after that. I've always enjoyed the game, but finally decided to do a full dive on the Steam version. Prior to this "session" I'd only seen the mech bosses. 

What a fun little gem. I used to tell people "its 2D Minecraft!" but its just *not* anymore. Terraria has evolved into its own thing several times over, starting with a similar experience but rapidly evolving into a boss rush with ever increasing rewards. The game does a wonderful job with *growth*, allowing you to feel stronger the more time you put into a given character. Characters also carry over between worlds, allowing you to have an entire network of worlds that interact with one another via the player. 

It's 10$, what are you waiting for?

I don't think I need to introduce Terraria. It's a content-rich 2D sandbox game where you gather resources, craft workstations to obtain better gear, and defeat monsters and bosses. Doing so unlocks new NPCs (your villagers or citizens) to buy items from, opening up new content to farm and new bosses to prepare for. Rinse and repeat.

Beyond the combat loop, it features multiple difficulty levels, world sizes, unique biomes, and endless sandbox gameplay. You can go fishing, build pretty housing (or a tiny prison) for your dear NPCs, and terraform the world to your liking, whether that means digging a massive hole straight into Hell, or building a (railway) bridge across the entire sky. It’s also quite fun in co-op if you want to bring friends along for the ride. Even though the devs keep teasing their "final update," there have been several "last updates" already, so new content just keeps magically appearing.

Little late for writing a review since I have over 200 hours in this game at the time I am writing this.

I am obsessed with this game! pre-boss is frustrating when starting a new character and world but I can't get enough of it since when you overcome it, it becomes rewarding.

I love the feeling of the power creep when you are getting boss drops, finding good weapons in chests, and being able to kill a mob/boss with ease that used to destroy you, It makes you feel so rewarded when you finally get the weapon/item/armor that makes you stronger.

Exploring is fun, I love being able to find lots of different areas underground and being able to loot structures that I encounter.

I love building (that's all I am going to say since building is fun.)

I wish there were lots of more content post moon lord, other then that I love this game!

As a fresh new player to Terraria, this game is awesome. It is actually pretty easy to get used to, and you learn as you progress. Some people say "It's just 2D Minecraft, what's the difference?" Well, Minecraft is more of a sandbox game. Meanwhile Terraria is kinda like one, but just not the same, because Terraria features bosses and some sort of progression to it, if you know what I mean. Anyways, Terraria is a wonderful and HEAVILY addicting game. so play at your own risk. 
10/10 game

This game is NOT 2D Minecraft. Total lie. I tried it, and the combat factor is totally awesome. This doesn't have a, "creative mode," but it has over 15 bosses to fight to your heart's content. You can't play 15 minutes  and say it stinks, progression is difficult! So when you overcome a challenge, satisfaction is there! Please try it when you have the chance.

Lots of room for modding and public servers. 10/10 game, most all issues are superficial or minute, and can be chalked up to preference or tedium.

A fantastic, big sandbox game in retro-pixel style, but it's a bit complicated to find out the controls and your next goals just by yourself. 
Best thing is that this game even gets new updates until today! It may offer some modding options, but in my opinions they are less appealing, because you would need to replace standard options for that (I'm referring to modding own hairs, didn't check out other modding-options). 
Crafting, building and collecting resources is fun, but the controls can become annoying if you have suddenly to switch from a tool to a weapon, just to defend yourself against the huge amount of spawning enemies. 
And you are able to do so many things in this game, like fishing, flying, fighting, building, exploring and you can even turn into a werewolf by night! If you should be disappointed by it's character creation: don't worry, you are going to unlock so many cosmetics and dyes later in the game, you are for sure able to dress up your hero to your own, personal liking! 
A game with so many varieties, which even offers so much play-time (81 hours for me and I still didn't beat the game yet completely) I can easily recommend to anyone and it's even more fun if you play it together with a/your friend(s)!

Terraria is an amazing game that offers endless possibilities for creativity, one of the things I like most about it is how much freedom it gives players to build and create anything they can imagine, you can design your own world, construct unique buildings, and explore countless ideas without limits.

The game also helps develop creativity because there are so many different materials, tools, and building options available, every world can look completely different depending on the player's imagination.

Another great aspect of Terraria is its atmosphere, the pixel-art graphics give the game a unique charm and make it feel both nostalgic and exciting, combined with the music, different biomes, and interesting enemies, the game creates a fantastic experience that keeps players engaged for hours.

Overall, I think Terraria is a fantastic game, It is fun, creative, and full of adventure I would highly recommend it to anyone who enjoys exploration, building, and using their imagination :)

This is one of the greastest indie games of all time and a true gem. For $9 the game gives so much. This game does exploration, progression, and rpg like combat better than any other sandbox including minecraft. Even though I still prefer minecraft for other things Terraria really does have different approach while sticking to the core of a good sandbox. 2d building is quite unique from 3d building and it is its own skill. What makes it approach unique is it gives the player much more control and traversal over a world than minecraft which is designed to be played one block at a time. Pick axe upgrades still to the ability to place and break as the main form of progression similar to other games but its the scale from begining to end thats satisfying. You go from breaking a block in 3 seconds to hundreds in 1 second. Biome spread becomes a minor problem with the clentamintor and it also gives you freedom what where you want biomes to be. The 2 dozen ore really make minning and exploring a large part of game and motivation behind getting strong gear. 

The game has 4 damage types across hundreds of wepons to have some rpg mechanics and keeps progession different and varried character to character. The accessories can have an even bigger impact on combat than the armor. The accessories and mounts drastically increase the mobility and traversal and it is a staple of its progression. Building world wide infrastructure like a rail setting up pylons or hellivator make world traversal quick. With enough skills almost any armor set and tons of wepons are viable for the boss fights. Despite building not being the biggest highlight it also done very well. Building a more helpful environment and unlocking more npcs is a big motivator for building. The happiness system also gets you to building in more places and expirment with new blocks. The pylons are prices are a fair reward for those that do or dont take advantage of it.
im going for 100% on this game but i have had so much fun with it, especially after not playing for years. definitely a little slow at the beginning but the exploration is fun when first experiencing

Across two different accounts, playing on mobile, Xbox, and Nintendo, playing both Terraria and tModLoader for PC. I've totaled around, maybe at the minimum of 4500+ hours on this game. Having a lot of experience and the love of the game, I like it! I recommend playing it, have fun with it, experience the different difficulties. try classic if you want a eased experience (or journey if you really wanna try building) or expert for a challenge, or master if you want to struggle while playing it for the first time. The QoL mods on tModLoader are, well nice! The big content mods like Calamity and Fargo's Souls Mod are really good. I definitely recommend!

Ignored this game for the past 15 years, and that was probably right thing to do. It's definitely not a "2D minecraft", at very least it tries not to be.

Progression feels nice, without too much of hand-holding. Idk about other difficulty levels, but Expert felt balanced enough. The world being giant sandbox really helps with this. You either tryhard next boss or do prep work (arena, potions, equipment, etc...), which incentivizes more world exploration, and exploration is rewarding.

But there are a few rough edges:

* Once you beat the final boss, there are very few activities left to do. Some seasonal events, extra stuff for achievements and that's it.
* You learn about cool stuff from the wiki. So 2010-ies. NPCs could do better at guiding you or telling about not-so-obvious game mechanics.
* Some achievements are mind-numbing. Trash Compactor is the worst offender, after angler(+related) ones.

Overall, this game feels mature enough (for the genre). If you don't go full autist-mode, there is a fun to have.

Ive actually played Terraria on Playstation & Xbox for over 1000 hours, so yeah I like this game a lot. Its more fun multiplayer, but there is a ton that you can do & explore & create. It might not be everyones cup of tea, but I like it. Its kind of like a 2d Minecraft with more attitude.

It's insane how much time you can spend in this without even realizing. For $10 200 hours of fun in just the base game is insane, and with mods you can easily squeeze out 2x more, even if you're not prone to spending a lot of time in 1 game.
